items.summary: The CMDA is executing the project to link Kilambakkam Bus Terminus with the new halt railway station in three phases at a cost of ₹79 cr. While the first girder has been installed on GST road, traffic clearance is awaited for the second, says official

items.title: Cauvery Water Management Authority is meeting on Wednesday amid a growing deficit in water realisation by Tamil Nadu
items.summary: Since June 1, the start of the current water year, four reservoirs of the Cauvery basin have received around 30 tmc ft, whereas Tamil Nadu has not even received four tmc ft, says an expert in Chennai

items.title: Tamil Nadu Law Minister R. Nirmalkumar justifies the arrest of DMK Vilathikulam MLA G.V. Markandayan
items.summary: He says the MLA spoke in a threatening manner. If an MLA goes to the extent of issuing a death threat to the Chief Minister, it is definitely a law and order issue. If someone speaks in this manner, legal action is bound to be taken.

items.summary: He was responding to a question raised by DMK MP P. Wilson in the Rajya Sabha

items.title: Tamil Nadu scales up ABHA creation, 2.54 crore people covered so far
items.summary: ABHA, a 14-digit unique health identifier created using an Aadhaar number, is a key feature of the Union government’s Ayushman Bharat Digital Mission (ABDM), launched in 2021, enabling a digital health records ecosystem

items.title: Highways Department to begin desilting of stormwater drains in Chennai soon
The MGR-Vijay parallel: ‘Jana Nayagan’ revives a rare moment in Tamil Nadu history
items.summary: When MGR became Chief Minister, ‘Meenava Nanban’ followed. Now, history repeats with Vijay’s ‘Jana Nayagan’, releasing on July 23

items.title: Bhaktavatsalam’s take on the destruction of documents relating to anti-Hindi agitation in Tamil Nadu
items.summary: He said he was advised that papers on politicians against whom detention orders had been served need not be kept so that they would not be available to the very people who were likely to take charge of the government. When Ministers came to know about officers who had made reports about them, they would get prejudiced, he further said

items.title: The Kumbakonam connection of MGR, M.K. Nambyar and M.S. Swaminathan
items.summary: The families of the iconic film star and former Tamil Nadu Chief Minister, the distinguished constitutional lawyer, and the celebrated agricultural scientist hailed from Kerala. But they spent a formative part of their lives in Kumbakonam, known for its rich cultural heritage, before rising to national prominence in politics, cinema, law, and science

items.title: Dolphin City: Chennai’s dolphinarium that shaped national debate on marine mammal captivity over 25 years ago
items.summary: Hosting a dolphinarium in Chennai turned into controversy as all the three dolphins died, setting off one of the country’s earliest debates on the ethics of marine mammal captivity

items.title: Why the Church of Scotland Mission was worried about schools in Madras falling into debt
items.summary: While notable progress was made in the area of female education, there was also concern over the lack of availability of female teachers to tutor the girls.

items.title: 170 years ago, a debate in Britain parliament on torture in Madras
items.summary: Forms of torture in Madras Presidency at the time included tying a man by the hair of his head to the tail of an ass, and parading him though the public market

items.title: Chennai’s iconic annual fair at Island Grounds turns 50
items.summary: The 70-day-long India Tourism and Industrial Fair that began at the Island Grounds in the city recently turned 50 this year. The Sutrula Porutkatchi was conceived and named by former Chief Minister M. Karunanidhi. It began in the city in 1974 as the ‘Pongal Tourist Trade Fair.’

items.title: Ram Setu vs. Sethusamudram project: When Tamil Nadu BJP headquarters was attacked by DMK cadres
items.summary: In response to DMK president M. Karunanidhi’s remark on Ram Setu, Vishwa Hindu Parishad (VHP) leader and former BJP MP Ram Vilas Vedanti triggered a major controversy in September 2007 by saying that “whosoever chopped off the head and tongue of the DMK president would be gifted in gold.”

items.title: An idol found near Gangaikonda Cholapuram confirms the ‘Thiruvarasu’ of Nathamunigal
items.summary: Near the Gangaikonda Cholapuram-Kumbakonam Road is a place called Sorgapallam or Sembodai where an idol of Srinivasa Perumal as once found half-buried under a tree. In the year 2000, another idol was found buried in a field. It was that of the first of the Sri Vaishnava Acharyas, who was said to have been at the place in his final hours

items.title: Thanjavur Big Temple inscriptions bring alive the fierce fight between Shivaji and Afzal Khan
items.summary: The inscriptions, written in Marathi on the south-western walls near the Vinayagar Temple, also describe the history of the Maratha kings and praise Danish missionary Rev. Friedrich Christian Schwartz. They also highlight how Serfoji sent his army and offered financial assistance to the British during the war against the Marudu brothers

items.title: S. Chellapandian: a Speaker with sound knowledge of British parliamentary practices
items.summary: S. Chellapandian tried to follow British parliamentary practices. In the opinion of former President R. Venkataraman, he knew legislative procedures “so thoroughly that he would not only enforce them but would also guide new members to learn the art”

items.title: J.C. Daniel, a Tamil dentist and martial artist, who became the father of Malayalam cinema
items.summary: Driven by his passion for cinema, Daniel sold his share of family property in Kanniyakumari district and borrowed money from friends and relatives to produce the first Malayalam silent film Vigathakumaran (The Lost Child). The film told the story of a child kidnapped by a plantation worker from Sri Lanka and later reunited with his family

items.title: When explosives were planted at police premises across Tamil Nadu in 1999
items.summary: A massive search operation after a low intensity explosion in Chennai revealed that bombs were placed in the Police Commissionerate, Prison Headquarters and other police establishments

items.title: Collection of names of animals as portrayed in Tamil classics
items.summary: The ancient Tamils employed a remarkable richness of vocabulary to refer to animals. H. Chithiraputhiran, a former professor at Tamil University, Thanjavur, has compiled a specialised dictionary of animals in classical literature to recover these words

items.title: Goddess Saraswati without veena: Sculptures of Pallava, Chola eras reveal an earlier iconography
items.summary: The veena, today regarded as inseparable from Saraswati’s identity, appears to have emerged as her dominant symbol only in the later period.

items.title: ‘Victory Pillar’ in honour of Operation Sindoor heroes getting ready near Swamimalai
items.summary: The pillar is being made of panchaloha by a team of highly skilled craftsmen near Swamimalai; casting ceremony held on Sunday

items.title: Farmers body urges the DMK to drop its demand to form Mekedatu tribunal
items.summary: P.R. Pandian, president of the Coordination Committee of All Farmers Associations of Tamil Nadu, says it will jeopardise the farmers of the State and is against the law and farmers’ interests
